# SL-PERF-002: google-cls-poor

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

PageSpeed or CrUX reports poor CLS for the page or origin.

## Metadata

- Category: Performance and technical signals (performance-technical)
- Default severity: warning
- Confidence: certain
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: google
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- google

## Required Evidence

- cls-value
- data-source
- observed-at
- fetched-at

## When This Triggers

Compare latest Google CLS observation with provider thresholds and freshness.

## False Positives

False positives are usually caused by stale or sampled external observations.
Check the observation date, freshness, and provider payload before suppressing.

## How To Fix

Investigate layout shifts and distinguish page-level from origin-level data.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/performance/google-cls-poor

Fixed direction:

- Investigate layout shifts and distinguish page-level from origin-level data.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

## Implementation Surfaces

- Google integration must provide dated, freshness-aware observations before
  this rule can use Google evidence.
